# **![][image1]ASAP CRN Cloud**

# **Dataset Description Document**

This document is designed to gather specific information from dataset contributors to accurately and comprehensively describe their dataset. Please fill out all relevant sections to the best of your ability.  **Pay particular attention to the accuracy, as the information in this document will become publicly visible through the dataset’s DOI and the ASAP CRN Cloud description card**. Please make a copy, fill in the appropriate details, and share back with [Matthieu Darracq](mailto:matthieu.darracq@dnastack.com) as a link, or emailed as a docx file.

**Bucket name:** asap-raw-team-vangheluwe-ipsc-bisulfseq-astro-atp13a2lof

# **Authorship Information**

Provide the name of the dataset authors for citation including Affiliations, and ORCID.  Add additional rows as necessary. These will be the citations in the DOI. Include as many or few as your lab policy dictates.

# **Dataset Details**

Please limit the use of acronyms to well-established ones and avoid team-specific branding.

| Dataset Title | DNA methylome profiling (Bisulfite Sequencing) of human iPSC-derived midbrain astrocytes in ATP13A2 c.1306deficiency models |
| ----: | :---- |
| **Dataset Description** | This dataset provides base-resolution DNA methylation profiles generated via Whole-Genome Bisulfite Sequencing (WGBS) of human iPSC-derived midbrain astrocytes. The study contrasts the methylome of ATP13A2 c.1306 mutants with Wild Type (WT) controls. The objective was to evaluate overall DNA methylation levels and regional epigenetic modifications associated with the genotype. This dataset is designed to be integrated with companion RNA-seq and ATAC-seq data to provide a multi-omic characterization of the model. |

# **Project Information**

Provide the name and a brief description of the overall project to which this dataset belongs.  These should match the STUDY.csv table metadata entries.

| Project Name | Implications of Polyamine and Glucosylceramide Transport for Parkinson’s Disease (IMPACT-PD) |
| ----: | :---- |
| **Project Description** | Our research program investigates the molecular mechanisms of Parkinson’s disease (PD) by dissecting how PD-associated genes—specifically ATP13A2, ATP10B, and GBA1—regulate lysosomal homeostasis. As part of the ASAP Collaborative Research Network (CRN), our team employs a multi-disciplinary approach—integrating human iPSC-derived midbrain models, rodent systems, and purified protein complexes—to determine how lysosomal dysfunction triggers cellular states associated with neurodegeneration. A primary focus of this project is characterizing the transition of astrocytes toward a reactive, pro-inflammatory state and the subsequent impact on dopaminergic neuron survival. By generating large-scale transcriptomic (RNA-seq), chromatin accessibility (ATAC-seq), and methylome (bisulfite sequencing) datasets, we aim to map the pathways linking genetic loss-of-function (e.g., ATP13A2 c.1306) to chronic neuroinflammation and impaired lysosomal proteolysis. Ultimately, this work seeks to identify central regulators of these pathogenic states to provide new therapeutic strategies for PD. |
| **ASAP team name** | TEAM-VANGHELUWE |
| **ASAP grant ID** | ASAP-024297 |
